feature_name: Network Security Group Support
notes: Network rule set default_action=Deny → network-level control PASS; SB not subnet-injected so NSG proxy via rule set | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/service-bus-security-baseline

feature_name: Virtual Network Integration
notes: Premium-only; non-Premium → UNKNOWN; Premium without VNet rules or private endpoints → FAIL | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/service-bus-security-baseline

feature_name: Azure Private Link
notes: At least one Approved private endpoint connection embedded in namespace object → PASS | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/service-bus-security-baseline

feature_name: Disable Public Network Access
notes: PASS if public_network_access=Disabled OR network rule set default_action=Deny | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/service-bus-security-baseline

feature_name: Monitor Anomalies and Threats Targeting Sensitive Data
notes: MCSB v3 baseline: False/Not Applicable. No native DLP solution for Service Bus message traffic. Phase 48 cache incorrectly marked as now_applicable_native — reverted. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/service-bus-security-baseline

feature_name: Encrypt Data in Transit
notes: minimum_tls_version in (1.2 / 1.3) → PASS | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/service-bus-security-baseline

feature_name: Encrypt Data at Rest with Platform-Managed Keys
notes: All SKUs (Basic/Standard/Premium) encrypted with MS-managed keys by default — auto-PASS (static) | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/service-bus-security-baseline

feature_name: Encrypt Data at Rest with Customer-Managed Key
notes: Premium SKU only; encryption.key_source=Microsoft.KeyVault → PASS; non-Premium → UNKNOWN; Premium without CMK → FAIL | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/service-bus-security-baseline

feature_name: Manage Cryptographic Keys using Key Management Service
notes: Premium SKU uses KV for CMK key management; ARM-checkable; non-Premium → UNKNOWN | Phase48-cache: Service Bus Premium supports CMK via Azure Key Vault (encryption.key_source=Microsoft.KeyVault). ARM-checkable. GA since 2020. [NEEDS-EXA-URL] Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/service-bus-messaging/configure-customer-managed-key

feature_name: Certificate Management using Azure Key Vault
notes: SB TLS certs are Microsoft-managed infrastructure; customer cert management via KV not ARM-readable → UNKNOWN

feature_name: Use Centralized Identity and Authentication System — Disable Local Auth
notes: Service Bus supports disabling local SAS key authentication, enforcing Entra ID-only access. Policy: 'Azure Service Bus should not use access keys for authentication'. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/service-bus-messaging/service-bus-authentication-and-authorization

feature_name: Use Centralized Identity and Authentication System — AAD Auth Required
notes: Azure AD authentication required for Service Bus data plane: True/True/Microsoft. Entra ID RBAC with built-in roles: Service Bus Data Owner, Data Sender, Data Receiver. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/service-bus-security-baseline

feature_name: Use Azure AD Managed Identities for Azure Resource Authentication
notes: namespace.identity present (SystemAssigned/UserAssigned) → PASS proxy; no identity → FAIL | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/service-bus-security-baseline

feature_name: Use Azure AD Service Principals for Azure Resource Authentication
notes: disable_local_auth=True → Entra/RBAC enforced → SP assignments supported → PASS proxy | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/service-bus-security-baseline

feature_name: Restrict Resource Access Based on Conditions — Conditional Access
notes: Conditional Access for Service Bus data plane = True/False/Customer per MCSB v3 baseline. Azure AD Conditional Access policies apply to Entra ID authentication for Service Bus. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/service-bus-security-baseline

feature_name: Restrict the Exposure of Credential and Secrets — Use Azure Key Vault
notes: MCSB v3 baseline: False/Not Applicable. Service Bus does not natively integrate credentials/secrets with Key Vault for IM-8. Note: DP-6 (CMK) IS supported for Premium tier via Key Vault — different feature. Phase 48 cache incorrectly marked as now_applicable_native — reverted. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/service-bus-security-baseline

feature_name: Enable Threat Detection Capabilities — Microsoft Defender for Service Bus
notes: No Defender for Service Bus product in Defender for Cloud portfolio; always UNKNOWN with rationale | MCSB v3 baseline: False/Not Applicable. No dedicated Microsoft Defender plan for Service Bus. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/service-bus-security-baseline

feature_name: Enable Logging for Azure Resources
notes: MonitorManagementClient.diagnostic_settings.list(ns.id); any enabled log category → PASS | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/service-bus-security-baseline

feature_name: Ensure Regular Automated Backups — Azure Backup Service
notes: Azure Backup does not support Service Bus; geo-DR is availability failover not data backup → UNKNOWN | PaaS managed service — backup is either not supported or managed differently from Azure Backup (e.g., geo-redundancy, snapshots). Feature not supported in MCSB v3 baseline for this service. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/backup/backup-overview

feature_name: Ensure Regular Automated Backups — Native Soft-Delete and Purge Protection
notes: Service Bus has no soft-delete or purge-protection for message data; entity config can be ARM-exported → UNKNOWN | PaaS managed service — backup is either not supported or managed differently from Azure Backup (e.g., geo-redundancy, snapshots). Feature not supported in MCSB v3 baseline for this service. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/backup/backup-overview

feature_name: Use Only Approved Azure Services — Azure Policy Support
notes: Tags presence as proxy for policy governance; empty tags → FAIL | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/service-bus-security-baseline

feature_name: Use Only Approved Applications in Virtual Machine — Defender for Cloud AAC
notes: PaaS — Adaptive Application Controls target VMs and Arc servers only | Network infrastructure resource with no customer-managed compute. Microsoft Defender for Servers/Endpoints is not applicable — no VMs are exposed to customers. Anti-malware is managed at the Azure infrastructure layer. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/infrastructure

feature_name: Separate and Limit Highly Privileged/Administrative Users — Disable Local Admin
notes: MCSB v3 baseline: False/Not Applicable. Service Bus has no local administrative account. All access via Entra ID RBAC or SAS tokens (latter should be disabled). Phase 48 cache incorrectly marked as now_applicable_native — reverted. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/service-bus-security-baseline

feature_name: Follow Just Enough Administration Principle — RBAC for Data Plane
notes: Azure RBAC for Service Bus data plane = True/False/Customer per MCSB v3 baseline. Built-in roles: Azure Service Bus Data Owner (full access), Data Sender (send only), Data Receiver (receive only). Scoped at namespace, queue, or topic subscription level. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/service-bus-messaging/authenticate-application

feature_name: Determine Access Process for Microsoft Support — Customer Lockbox
notes: MCSB v3 baseline: Customer Lockbox = False/Not Applicable. Service Bus not in Customer Lockbox supported services list. Phase 48 cache incorrectly marked as now_applicable_native — reverted. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/customer-lockbox-overview
